tldr: Move from Yandex's free MMP to ByteBrew's free game-focused analytics + attribution stack.
why_switch: Reduce vendor and geopolitical exposure tied to Yandex ownership
Get a stack purpose-built for mobile games (IAP, ad revenue, remote config)
Unity and Unreal SDKs with quick setup for game studios
All-in-one telemetry: analytics + attribution + remote config in one free tier
Avoid uncertain AppMetrica roadmap for Western markets
what_to_export: Raw event logs and user-level event streams
Install and attribution records (campaign, network, click/impression timestamps)
Crash and ANR reports if you rely on them
Funnel and cohort definitions for re-creation
Push notification audiences and segment definitions
Custom event schemas and parameter dictionaries
User profile and device identifier mappings
export_method: Use AppMetrica's Logs API and report exports to pull historical raw events, installs, and attribution data into your own storage (S3, BigQuery, etc.) before cutting over. Confirm the exact endpoints and quotas in the official AppMetrica documentation, since SDK and export feature sets evolve.
import_method: ByteBrew is primarily designed for forward-looking telemetry collected via its Unity, Unreal, or native SDKs rather than backfill. Integrate the ByteBrew SDK in a new app build and let it begin collecting events. For historical context, keep your AppMetrica export in a warehouse and join it with ByteBrew exports downstream. Consult ByteBrew docs for any supported server-side ingestion paths.

concept_mapping.note: Re-define event names and parameters in ByteBrew schema
Re-wire networks and postbacks via ByteBrew dashboard
Manual recreation; feature parity not guaranteed
ByteBrew is growth-focused; pick a dedicated push tool

concept_mapping.target: Dedicated crash tool (e.g. native or third-party)
concept_mapping.note: Verify ByteBrew crash coverage in current docs
Re-create config keys and A/B test groups

gotchas: Historical attribution will not transfer cleanly; treat ByteBrew as a fresh baseline
Push notifications and crash reporting may need separate vendors
ByteBrew is young; check current SDK/feature coverage before committing
Re-onboard ad networks and update postback URLs to ByteBrew
Validate iOS SKAdNetwork handling on the new SDK before launch

email.subject: Migrating mobile analytics from AppMetrica to ByteBrew
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving our mobile analytics and attribution from AppMetrica to ByteBrew to consolidate game telemetry and reduce vendor risk.

Plan:
1. Export raw events, installs, and attribution from AppMetrica into our warehouse for historical reference.
2. Integrate the ByteBrew SDK in the next app build and recreate our custom events, funnels, and remote config.
3. Re-wire ad network postbacks to ByteBrew and run both SDKs briefly for validation.

Expected effort: 1-3 days. Historical reports will live in the warehouse; new dashboards will be in ByteBrew.

Questions welcome.
